arts.ttl: Moratti reveals reasons for selling Inter and his message to Lautaro
arts.sum: Former Inter Milan president Massimo Moratti recently opened up about his decision to sell the club and shared his thoughts on current star striker Lautaro Martinez. In an interview with Radio Anch’io Sport (via Calciomercato), Moratti provided insights into the challenges he faced and the future of the club. Moratti explained that his decision to sell Inter Milan was primarily driven by financial considerations. He stated, “I sold Inter because the club needed significant investments that I couldn’t afford at the time. The football landscape had changed, and it was no longer possible for a single family to sustain such The post Moratti reveals reasons for selling Inter and his message to Lautaro appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Conte: ‘Napoli similar to when I was at Inter: We’re rebuilding’
arts.sum: Napoli manager Antonio Conte expressed satisfaction with his team’s performance following their victory against Palermo. Speaking after the 5-0 win, Conte highlighted the team’s approach and determination. “The approach to the game was important. I liked the desire, the determination, even the nastiness. We wanted to advance to the next round and we told ourselves that before the start,” Conte stated. However, he emphasized the need for continuous improvement, adding, “But there will always be work to do to improve some flaws.” When asked about standout players, Conte refrained from singling out individuals, preferring to focus on the team’s collective The post Conte: ‘Napoli similar to when I was at Inter: We’re rebuilding’ appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Inter’s defensive dilemma: Pavard’s form dips as Bisseck rises
arts.sum: Inter Milan manager Simone Inzaghi finds himself at a crossroads as he contemplates changes to his defensive lineup. The recent derby defeat against AC Milan has highlighted concerns, particularly regarding Benjamin Pavard’s form. Calciomercato report how the French defender has been struggling to find his best performance levels, with his difficulties evident not only in the Milan derby but also in the previous match against Monza. Pavard’s poor showing in the action leading to Dany Mota’s goal for Monza has raised eyebrows and prompted serious reflection from Inzaghi. arts.ttl: Capello: “Inter have become complacent and Inzaghi makes too much turnover”
arts.sum: Fabio Capello, the renowned former coach, has shared his insights on the current state of Italy’s top football clubs. His analysis for Gazzetta dello Sport covers the recent performances and challenges faced by Inter Milan, AC Milan, Juventus, and Napoli: “Tomorrow evening, Milan will play at home against Lecce.
